notes: Cross-session memory for the AV Intelligence Digest Agent. Read at session start (Task 0), written back at session end (Task 7). Stories are removed when last_status contains 'Resolved' and last_updated is more than 7 days ago.

ongoing_stories.headline: China nationwide AV permit freeze
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active — no resumption timeline confirmed as of June 1, 2026. MIIT suspended all new AV permits following Apollo Go system-wide outage. Freeze blocks fleet additions, new test programmes, and new city expansions for all operators. Existing services continue to operate.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Outage trigger date disputed — March 31, 2026 cited by EVWire/DIGITIMES as initial localized Wuhan incident; April 27, 2026 cited by Bloomberg/Automotive World as system-wide recurrence that triggered the national freeze. Most likely explanation: March 31 was a localized Wuhan event; April 27 was a system-wide recurrence that prompted the MIIT action on April 29. Root cause not publicly disclosed by Baidu. Human verification recommended against Chinese-language official sources.
status_change_triggers: Official MIIT or Ministry of Transport announcement on resumption or extension
Any operator receiving a new AV permit
Any official timeline given or revised
Any additional operator sanctioned or penalized
Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the disputed outage trigger date
Any regulatory document citing a specific confirmed date for the triggering outage

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo NHTSA recall — 3,791 vehicles, flooding defect
ongoing_stories.last_status: Open — interim OTA fix deployed (tighter weather restrictions, updated map data) but no permanent fix submitted to NHTSA. NHTSA has flagged in writing that no permanent fix has been received. Ongoing flooding incidents in Texas and Atlanta through late May resulted in additional service suspensions in those cities. San Antonio resumption expected imminently pending final NHTSA remedy.
status_change_triggers: Permanent fix submitted to or approved by NHTSA
NHTSA closes or escalates the investigation
Waymo issues a new interim fix or OTA update
San Antonio or Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo freeway ride suspension — construction zone edge cases
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing — surface streets operational in SF, LA, Phoenix, Miami. All freeway rides suspended after viral video showed vehicle navigating through highway construction cones; Waymo cited need to integrate technical learnings. Atlanta separately paused for flooding. Company indicated resumption 'soon' but no date given. Root cause not publicly disclosed.
status_change_triggers: Official freeway resumption announced by Waymo
Root cause publicly disclosed by Waymo
Software fix confirmed deployed via OTA

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan service shutdown — ongoing post-outage
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing — Apollo Go service in Wuhan remains shut down following the mass vehicle immobilization incident. No resumption date announced. All other Apollo Go cities continue operating under existing permits.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: See china-permit-freeze entry for disputed dates of the triggering outage event.
status_change_triggers: Official Apollo Go or Baidu announcement of Wuhan service resumption
Regulatory approval granted for Wuhan operations to restart
Root cause of outage publicly disclosed by Baidu
